profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:41:13 UTC.

ead.archdesc:
======= RMM08055Larry Clark archive8055Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections2B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:06:41 UTC.Describing Archives: A Content Standard >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Larry Clark archive
Clark, Larry, 1943-
8055
2800 items.
circa 1968-2011. In English. 7542019
Scope and content
Photographs, slides, posters, books, shirts and skateboard decks created by Larry Clark. Photographs are from projects including Tulsa, Teenage Lust, KIDS, and Larry Clark 1992, among others.
Preferred Citation
Larry Clark Archive, #8055. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Lawrence Donald "Larry" Clark was born in Tulsa, Oklahoma in 1943 and has worked as a film director, photographer, writer, and film producer best known for the movie Kids and the photography book Tulsa. His work documents illegal drug use, underage sex and violence, and especially youth who are part of specific subcultures like surfing, punk rock or skateboarding.
Separated Materials
10 books cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections.
Teenagers -- Drama. Teenagers -- Drug use. Drug addicts -- Oklahoma -- Tulsa -- Pictorial works. Skateboarding -- Pictorial works. Teenage boys -- United States -- Social conditions. Punk rock music.
7542019 <<<<<<< HEAD Photographs by Larry ClarkClark, Larry, 1943-1962-2011
Arrangement
Photographs are arranged by the project with which they were published. Photographs related to similar prints in published projects are included in that series. Some photographs appear in multiple publications, these were arranged with the earliest publication. Photographs where the project wasn't clear were arranged by date.Tulsa1962-1971
Arrangement
See Teenage Lust for more prints related to Tulsa, as some from this series were published only in this later publication.Gelatin silver prints.Published in Tulsa1962-197125.4 x 20.5 cm and smaller11-4Published in Tulsa1962-197130.5 x 21 cm351Jack and Lynn Johnson, Oklahoma City197327.7 x 35.5 cmMatted photograph3523Related to Tulsa1962-197125.4 x 20.5 cm and smaller15-6Related to Tulsa1962-197135.5 x 28 cm352-3Rejected prints1962-197135.5 x 28 cm359-10
Scope and Contents
Title applied by Clark. These photographs were removed from an Ilford paper box originally labeled "old rejects". Possibly prints rejected for exhibition.Teenage Lust1962-1983

Signed by Larry Clark.Grand Prix Festival du film Policier1999172 x 119.8 cm4The KIDS are coming1995156.7 x 101.6 cm5Another Day in Paradise, un film de Larry Clark1999162.5 x 119 cm6Film and exhibition posters1992-2009123Photographs from the collection of Larry Clark8Ralph Gibson printsGibson, Ralph, 1939-1968-197428 x 35.5 cm; 13.5 x 20.5 cm82
Scope and Contents
Three photographs from Gibson's "The Somnombulist" photo book and one photograph from "Deja-Vu". One photograph includes inscription to Larry Clark.
Related Materials
Additional photographs by Ralph Gibson are held in the collection of the Herbert F. Johnson Museum of Art, Cornell University.Gelatin silver prints.Young farmersSander, August191428 x 23.2 cm83
